name: Character Desire and Contradiction
description: Build characters around a core contradiction — what they want vs. what they need — and let the story force it to the surface.

name: Conceptual Metaphor as Structure
description: Let the SF core concept also be the literal embodiment of the story's theme — concept and emotional arc as one.

name: Defamiliarization
description: Make the familiar strange by shifting the observation angle — SF's unique cognitive reversal ability.

name: Dialogue Subtext
description: Write dialogue where the real meaning lives below the surface — characters talk about one thing but communicate another.

name: Earned Ending
description: Construct an ending that feels both surprising and inevitable — resolves the thematic question in a way the reader didn't predict.

name: Free Indirect Discourse
description: Blend third-person narration with a character's inner voice — slide between narrator authority and character subjectivity.

name: Iceberg Principle
description: Know 100% of your story's world, show only 10-20% on the page — the unseen mass gives the visible surface its gravity.

name: Image Systems
description: Create a network of recurring images that accumulate meaning across a story — theme expressed through pattern, not statement.

name: Incluing
description: Convey worldbuilding through character behavior, assumptions, and casual references instead of exposition dumps.

name: Novum Extrapolation Chain
description: From one core novum, systematically derive 2nd, 3rd, 4th order consequences — SF's core ideation tool.

name: Objective Correlative
description: Externalize internal emotion through a concrete object, scene, or chain of events — make the reader feel without telling.

name: POV Camera Discipline
description: Control what the reader sees by treating POV as a camera with strict rules — what it can show, what it must hide, when to cut.

name: Psychic Distance
description: Control how close or far the reader feels from a character's consciousness using Gardner's five-level spectrum.

name: Scene-Level Tension
description: Maintain reader engagement line-by-line through micro-tension — small unresolved questions, withheld information, disrupted expectations.

name: Sentence Rhythm
description: Control pace, emphasis, and emotional texture through deliberate variation in sentence length, structure, and sound.

name: Strategic Withholding
description: Deliberately leave key information out — the gaps the reader fills with imagination are more powerful than anything written.

name: Telling Detail
description: Select one or two hyper-specific details to stand in for an entire scene, character, or setting.
